english: pf. noṅs (nongs), to commit a fault, to make a mistake, to commit one's self, ći noṅs (ci nongs) what have I done amiss? bdág ma nóṅs-par ¿di-ltar gnód-pa bgyis (bdag ma nongs par 'di ltar gnod pa bgyis) I have thus been injured without my fault Dzl.; nóṅs-pa (nongs pa) fault, crime, nóṅs(-pa) mi byéd-pa (nongs(-pa) mi byed pa) not to commit a fault or crime Dzl.; bzód-pa (bzod pa) to pardon, to forgive, v. bzód-pa (bzod pa); nóṅs-pa bzód-par gsól-ba (nongs pa bzod par gsol ba) to ask pardon for a fault committed (in C. even: *nóṅ-pa sol-wa* (*nong pa sol wa*)); nóṅs-pa-ćan (nongs pa can) culpable, liable to punishment; *noṅ-ćan-ni(s) pe-ra* (*nong can ni(s) pe ra*) W. a reprehensible speech.
resp. no more alive, dead Dzl., rje-btsún sku ma nóṅs-par pébs-pa (rje btsun sku ma nongs par pebs pa) that your Reverence has arrived safe and sound Mil.
pf. and imp. mnos (mnos), to receive instruction, directions, favours, from a superior, esp. priest, Dzl., Glr.; but also to receive punishment.

I. also gnón-pa (gnon pa), pf. gnan (gnan), mnan (mnan), 1. to press, *mán-po ma non* (*man po ma non*) do not press too hard! *nán-te pé-će* (*nan te pe ce*) W. to open a thing by pressing; with or without rkáṅ-pas (rkang pas) to tread under foot, to crush; to pour over, to cover with, sas (sas), byé-mas (bye mas), with earth, with sand; to be drenched, ćár-pas (char pas) by a shower of rain Dzl.; to lay over, to overlay with Tar. 9, 11, 21; more frq. fig. to oppress, suppress, overcome, conquer, humble, keep under, mtó-ba khríms-kyis (mtho ba khrims kyis) the great people by laws Glr.; enemies frq.; evil spirits by magic, e.g. srí gnán-pa (sri gnan pa) by burying heads of animals in the ground, in order that the evil spirits may remain shut up there; bgégs nón-pa (bgegs non pa) to keep the spirits away from the fields during harvest by hatchets etc. stuck in the ground; po. Ká-bai gdóṅ srí mnan (Kha ba'i gdong sri mnan) I have crushed, subdued, the face of the snow (i. e. its surface) that was adverse to me Mil.; sa gnón-du (sa gnon du) the sitting posture of a saint, when his left hand rests in his lap, and his right hand hangs down, keeping down, as it were, the earth and her powers; cf. mnyam-bzhág (mnyam bzhag). — Frq. also: mya-ṅán-gyis (mya ngan gyis), snyíṅ-rjes (snying rjes) etc. to be overcome by misery, by compassion. — 2. to overtake, to catch, to reach, bdás-pas (bdas pas) in the pursuit Mil. and W. — 3. sgo-ṅa (sgo nga) to brood, to hatch, eggs, Sch. II. W. lo tsam-non (lo tsam non), for lon (lon), how old is he?
pf. noms (noms), 1. Cs. to be satisfied, contented (ṅóm-pa? (ngom pa?)) — 2. to seize, to lay hold of (snóm-pa (snom pa)); Sch.: noms-nyúg byéd-pa (noms nyug byed pa).

I. (Ssk. धन, also वसु) 1. wealth, property, possessions, nor(-la) gód-pa (nor(-la) god pa) Mil. to suffer a loss of property; *nor gód-da* (*nor god da*) or *póg-ga* (*pog ga*) W. have you suffered damage or loss? *nor nyams ćó-pa* (*nor nyams co pa*) C., *lén-će* (*len ce*) W., to examine the inventory, the amount of property; págs-pai nor bdun (pags pa'i nor bdun) Mil. the seven (spiritual) possessions of a saint, v. Tríg. 17; proverb: *ráṅ-nor-la man mi-nor-la dhug (sc. tar to)* (*rang nor la man mi nor la dhug (sc. tar to)*) look upon your own property as a medicine, upon that of others as a poison; thing, substance, much the same as rdzas (rdzas), Zam. (nif.). — 2. more or less exclusively: money, nór-la ltá-ba (nor la lta ba) to care for money, to be avaricious, easily bribed etc.; nor skyí-ba (nor skyi ba) to borrow money, nor bsri-ba (nor bsri ba) to save money, to scrape together; nor sog-jóg-pa (nor sog jog pa) to accumulate riches. — 3. Sch.: cattle, even in such phrases as: nor-khríg-pa (nor khrig pa) the pairing of cattle. Sch., nor-dpon (nor dpon) Desg. chief neatherd (provincialism of C.?). — 4. heritage, inheritance, bkó-ba (bko ba) to divide (it among the heirs); pá-nor (pa nor) heritage from the father, má-nor (ma nor) heritage from the mother. — 5 symb. num.: 8 (cf. nór-lha (nor lha)). Comp. nór-skal (nor skal) inheritance, hereditary portion; nór-skal-rnams (nor skal rnams) funds, capital Mil. — nor-rgyún (nor rgyun) imperishable riches Cs.; nor-rgyún-ma (nor rgyun ma) a goddess, nor-ćan (nor can) wealthy, opulent, rich Cs. — nór-bdag (nor bdag) 1. a man of wealth. 2. an heir. 3. a money-changer, usurer, Hind. महाजन, nór-bdag-mo (nor bdag mo) fem. of it; also n. of a goddess; nór-bdag-bu (nor bdag bu) heir. — nór-dus (nor dus) Pur. the gathering of taxes. — nór-brnab-ćan (nor brnab can) covetous, greedy of money. — nor-phyúgs (nor phyugs) amount, or stock of cattle, nor-¿brú (nor 'bru) store of corn. — nór-bu (nor bu) v. that article.
